mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In high-inflation environments where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, further increases in the federal funds rate trigger immediate equity sell-offs due to recession fears, yielding negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are non-negative following FEDFUNDS hikes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the negative correlation vanishes during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth consistently coincides with negative SPX 20-day returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction and reduced household income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS drops below negative 50,000, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where RSAFS outpaces GDPC1 momentum, indicates robust consumer-driven demand that lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth stalls below 0.5%, manufacturing weakness amplifies recession expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO growth falls below 0.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance during periods of stable employment.
